avalancheActivity.highlights: New snow and wind slabs in all aspects. New snow represents the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: As a consequence of new snow and a strong to storm force northeasterly wind, sometimes large wind slabs formed by the evening in particular in places that are protected from the wind. High-altitude starting zones have been replenished further quickly. In all aspects medium-sized slab avalanches are possible as a consequence of the snowfall. In particular on wind-loaded slopes the avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack and reach large size.

snowpackStructure.comment: The old snowpack is moist. The new snow can be released naturally.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a strong to storm force wind from northerly directions, extensive wind slabs formed in the last few days also in areas not adjacent to ridgelines. Along the border with France and along the border with Switzerland these avalanche prone locations are more prevalent.
In some localities the wind slabs will increase in size moderately as the day progresses. They can be released, caution is to be exercised at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible in isolated cases.
Dry avalanches can reach medium size.
Backcountry touring calls for caution and restraint.

snowpackStructure.comment:
20 to 30 cm of snow, but less in some localities, fell on Thursday above approximately 2000 m.
The wind was strong to storm force. The wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly. Over a wide area snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is soft and has a loosely bonded surface, in particular in places that are protected from the wind.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The meteorological conditions will facilitate a gradual strengthening of the near-surface layers in particular on sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: For those venturing off piste a sometimes precarious avalanche situation will be encountered over a wide area.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a storm force northerly wind, sometimes large wind slabs formed in all aspects. These can be released very easily above the tree line. Avalanches can be triggered in near-surface layers. Additionally in some places avalanches can penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 1800 m.
Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger.

In addition dry loose snow avalanches are possible, in the event of solar radiation especially on very steep slopes.

Restraint is advisable on this first sunny day.

snowpackStructure.comment:
10 to 25 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Wednesday.
The wind was violent in some cases. The wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly.

The new snow and wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack above the tree line. The old snowpack will be weakly bonded, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 1800 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: Considerable avalanche danger will persist. The avalanches can in some places be released easily.

avalancheActivity.comment:
The storm force wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly.
The hard wind slabs of the last three days are lying on a crust in particular on sunny slopes and at high altitude. These can be released by a single winter sport participant. In particular on steep north, northeast and northwest facing slopes the avalanches can be triggered in the faceted old snow and reach quite a large size.


Caution is to be exercised on wind-loaded slopes, and in gullies and bowls.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some regions 10 to 15 cm of snow, and up to 20 cm in some localities, fell on Wednesday.
The foehn wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly. Over a wide area wind slabs are lying on a hard crust.
In particular adjacent to ridgelines at high altitude snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

As a consequence of low temperatures the snowpack can not consolidate, in particular on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The solar radiation will give rise to gradual consolidation of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger remains high, as new drifts of wind-blown snow have formed in many places and are prone to avalanches.
avalancheActivity.comment: As a consequence of new snow and a sometimes storm force wind from northerly directions, wind slabs formed in the last two days in all aspects. They are bonding only slowly with the old snowpack. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Also transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are especially precarious. The fresh wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant. Apart from the danger of being buried, restraint should be exercised as well in view of the danger of avalanches sweeping people along and giving rise to falls.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack will be subject to considerable local variations. The wind has extensively reshaped the snowpack, causing the recent surface snow to be blown away and creating extensive eroded areas interspersed with areas of accumulation; in many areas, there are hard, icy surface crusts, some of which are hidden. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights:
The weather will be cloudy.
As a consequence of a moderate wind from northerly directions, further wind slabs will form.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of a sometimes storm force wind from northerly directions, wind slabs formed in the last two days in all aspects. They are bonding only slowly with the old snowpack. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. The fresh wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant.
Weak layers exist in the old snowpack on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. Avalanches can be triggered in the old snowpack reach large size in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The small quantity of fresh snow and in particular the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes. Avalanches can in some places be released in deep layers in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The wind slabs represent the main danger. Adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls and on steep slopes dry slab avalanches are possible, even large ones in isolated cases. Avalanches can be released by small loads. This applies in particular above the tree line, as well as at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.

Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack on very steep shady slopes. These avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in northwest to north to northeast facing aspects. Medium-sized avalanches are possible especially adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. These can release deeper layers of the snowpack and reach quite a large size. Slopes that have been little used this winter thus far where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially dangerous. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The sometimes storm force wind has transported the new snow significantly. As a consequence of a strong to storm force wind from northerly directions, sometimes large wind slabs formed in the last two days in particular in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. The fresh wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack in all aspects and generally above the tree line.
Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on steep shady slopes. The solar radiation will give rise on Saturday to softening of the snowpack on sunny slopes below approximately 2000 m.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The more recent wind slabs represent the main danger. Dry slab avalanches are to be expected. They can be released by small loads. Medium-sized avalanches are possible on wind-loaded slopes. This applies in all aspects above approximately 1800 m, caution is to be exercised in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls.
Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack on very steep shady slopes. These avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in northwest to north to northeast facing aspects, caution is to be exercised in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls, as well as at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack. They can also release deeper layers of the snowpack. Medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large avalanches are possible.
Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The sometimes storm force wind has transported the new snow significantly. As a consequence of a strong to storm force wind from northerly directions, sometimes large wind slabs formed in the last two days in particular in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. The fresh wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack in all aspects and generally above the tree line.
Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on steep shady slopes. The solar radiation will give rise on Saturday to softening of the snowpack on sunny slopes below approximately 2000 m.

avalancheActivity.highlights: New snow and wind slabs in the early morning. The new snow represents the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: As a consequence of new snow and a sometimes storm force wind, sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs formed in the course of the day in all aspects. In particular on very steep slopes individual small and medium sized avalanches are possible as the snowfall becomes more intense.

snowpackStructure.comment: The new snow will be deposited on the quite favourable surface of an old snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Outside marked and open pistes an unfavourable avalanche situation will prevail.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The extensive wind slabs are very prone to triggering in all aspects above the tree line. These can be released in near-surface layers and reach medium size. Avalanches can also penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size on west, north and east facing slopes. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and fresh avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger.

As a consequence of solar radiation loose snow avalanches are possible. This applies in particular on very steep slopes in places that are protected from the wind.

snowpackStructure.comment:
20 to 50 cm of snow fell in the last few days.
The wind was violent in some cases. The northwesterly wind has transported the new snow and, in some cases, old snow as well. Extensive wind slabs formed.

The new snow and wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack above the tree line. The old snowpack will be weakly bonded, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: For those venturing off piste a precarious avalanche situation will be encountered over a wide area.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a storm force northerly wind, sometimes large wind slabs formed in all aspects. These can be released very easily above the tree line. Avalanches can be triggered in near-surface layers. Additionally in some places avalanches can penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m.
The danger exists primarily in alpine snow sports terrain. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger.

In addition dry loose snow avalanches are possible, in the event of solar radiation especially on extremely steep slopes.

Restraint is advisable on this first sunny day.

snowpackStructure.comment:
10 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Wednesday.
The wind was violent in some cases. The wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly.

The new snow and wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack above the tree line. The old snowpack will be weakly bonded, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: Avalanches can as before be released easily.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and wet snow represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In particular on steep slopes individual small to medium-sized slab avalanches are possible above approximately 1400 m.
In the vicinity of peaks a dangerous avalanche situation will be encountered in some localities. The more recent wind slabs and can be released easily.
Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach medium size in some cases.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The small quantity of fresh snow and in particular the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.comment:
Wind slabs can be released easily by a single winter sport participant in all aspects above approximately 1800 m. Avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. This also applies at a distance from ridgelines, as well as in places that are protected from the wind. Avalanches are medium-sized.

In isolated cases avalanches can also penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack. Caution is to be exercised in particular on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
10 to 15 cm of snow fell in the last few days. The northwesterly wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly. As a consequence of a gusty wind, avalanche prone wind slabs formed.

The new snow and wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack above approximately 1800 m. The old snowpack will be weakly bonded, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: Avalanches can be released by a single winter sport participant.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The northwesterly wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly. Wind slabs can in some places be released by a single winter sport participant and reach medium size, caution is to be exercised at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.
Backcountry touring calls for caution.

snowpackStructure.comment:
5 to 10 cm of snow, but less in some localities, fell on Thursday above approximately 2000 m.
The wind was strong to storm force. The wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly. Over a wide area snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is soft and has a loosely bonded surface. This applies in places that are protected from the wind.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The meteorological conditions will facilitate a gradual strengthening of the near-surface layers in particular on sunny slopes.

regions.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: Fresh wind slabs are to be evaluated with care and prudence.
avalancheActivity.comment: Wind slabs are in some cases prone to triggering in particular on west to north to east facing aspects above the tree line. Avalanches can in very isolated cases penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach medium size.

snowpackStructure.comment: The wind was storm force in some cases. The northwesterly wind has transported some snow. Avalanche prone wind slabs formed.

tendency.highlights: Fresh wind slabs are to be evaluated with care and prudence.

avalancheActivity.highlights: More slab avalanches are possible, even medium-sized ones.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The northwesterly wind has transported the loosely bonded old snow. Wind slabs have formed in particular on northeast, east and south facing slopes.
Wind slabs can in isolated cases be released, even by a single winter sport participant and reach medium size. This applies in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example, and on wind-loaded slopes at elevated altitudes.

Unfavourable are steep slopes where recent wind slubs lie on melt-freeze crusts or smooth hardened surfaces and steep shady slopes where weak layers exist in the top section of the old snowpack.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The northwesterly wind has transported the loosely bonded old snow. As a consequence of the strong foehn wind, snow drift accumulations formed during the last three days.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Over a wide area wind slabs are lying on a hard crust.
On sunnier slopes where temperatures are less cold, the hard surface of the snowpack will gradually soften over the course of the day.

Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of new snow and wind the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase on Sunday.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The wind slabs are to be assessed with care and prudence.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The northwesterly wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly. The hard wind slabs are lying on a crust in particular on sunny slopes and at high altitude.
These can in isolated cases be released, even by a single winter sport participant and reach medium size. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in gullies and bowls and on wind-loaded slopes. Along the border with France the likelihood of avalanches is higher.

In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.

Backcountry touring calls for caution.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The foehn wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly. Over a wide area wind slabs are lying on a hard crust, especially on sunny slopes in all altitude zones, and adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas at elevated altitudes.

High altitudes shady slopes: Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted.
Individual weak layers exist in the centre of the snowpack especially on west, north and northeast facing slopes.

On sunnier slopes where temperatures are less cold, the hard surface of the snowpack will gradually soften over the course of the day.

In particular adjacent to ridgelines at high altitude snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and wet snow represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In the vicinity of peaks a dangerous avalanche situation will be encountered in some localities. The more recent wind slabs are mostly small and can in some cases be released easily.
As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Above approximately 1500 m small moist and wet avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack will be in most cases moist. The small quantity of fresh snow and in particular the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
